blocks.content: The summer Millhaven almost burned down started with three dry weeks and Kit Jenkins deciding Wednesday morning was a good time to find out how fast you could take the Sycamore Street hill without braking.
They'd nearly taken out Mrs. Dupont's azaleas at the bottom.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I want you to know I said no.
Kit pulled his bike out of the ditch. Eli picked gravel out of his palm.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: You almost didn't have knees anymore.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: But I do. Currently. Still have them.
It was already in the mid-eighties and not yet nine.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: We're going to melt before noon.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Georgia in June. That's just weather.
Eli wiped his face on his sleeve. Kit was already back on his bike, hands loose on the grips.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'll know when we get there.
Kit pushed off down the road.
Eli stood there a second, looking down the hill at the azalea bush that was still missing a branch.
Then he got on his bike.
